description: When parallel-slot merge-back hits a non-progress conflict, spawn Claude in slot 0's worktree to resolve it instead of resetting and abandoning the slot's commits. Progress files are already handled cheaply by git's union driver (shipped pre-PRD). Adds a resolver-callback seam in worktree.rs so unit tests can exercise outcomes without spawning Claude, and a real implementation that calls spawn_claude with Auto permissions in the conflicted slot 0 worktree.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output for touched files
Rust: All tests pass in scoped `cargo test` for touched modules
No breaking changes to existing public APIs without explicit need
All new functions have a single-paragraph rustdoc explaining intent + failure modes
Errors carry git stderr/stdout context, not just generic strings
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio)
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001
Resolver is a callback — production wires Claude, tests inject deterministic mocks
Failures must reset slot 0 cleanly; never leave the worktree in a half-merged state

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no panic' without verifying outcomes (merged_slots vs failed_slots membership, HEAD position)
Mock resolvers that bypass MERGE_HEAD / HEAD inspection — the inspection logic is the contract, not the resolver itself
Catch-all error handlers that swallow git stderr instead of formatting it into the failed_slots diagnostic
Spawning Claude with Dangerous permission mode — Auto with Read/Edit/Bash is the ceiling
Leaving MERGE_HEAD set after a failed resolution — every error path must reset to pre_merge_head
Hardcoding the task-mgr binary path in any new code — current_exe() with a labeled fallback only
Editing any tasks/*.json file directly during loop iterations

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver` exists and accepts a `&dyn MergeResolver`; existing `merge_slot_branches` delegates to it with a no-op resolver and unchanged behavior on conflict (reset + failed_slots)
Positive: `MergeResolverOutcome` enum has exactly the three variants Resolved, Aborted, Failed(String)
Positive: Helpers `list_conflicted_files`, `has_unresolved_merge`, `rev_parse_head` exist as pub(crate) and use `git` Command invocations (no shelling out via `sh`)

Positive: On non-zero merge exit, resolver is called with the conflicted file list and ephemeral branch name; on resolver Resolved, the function verifies MERGE_HEAD is cleared AND HEAD advanced before pushing to merged_slots
Negative: When resolver returns Resolved but MERGE_HEAD is still set, slot is in failed_slots with diagnostic 'resolver claimed resolution but MERGE_HEAD remains' and slot 0 HEAD == pre_merge_head
Negative: When resolver returns Resolved but HEAD did not advance, slot is in failed_slots with 'resolution did not advance HEAD'
Unit test: With mock resolver returning Resolved + side-effecting a real commit on a fixture repo → slot in merged_slots, HEAD advanced, MERGE_HEAD absent
Unit test: With mock resolver returning Aborted + running real `git merge --abort` → slot in failed_slots with 'declined' diagnostic, HEAD == pre_merge_head
Unit test: With mock resolver returning Failed("timed out") → slot in failed_slots, HEAD reset to pre_merge_head, diagnostic contains 'timed out'
Unit test: With mock resolver returning Resolved but doing nothing (no commit, MERGE_HEAD untouched) → slot in failed_slots, HEAD reset to pre_merge_head, diagnostic mentions MERGE_HEAD
Unit test: Existing test_merge_slot_branches_merges_disjoint_changes still passes (back-compat wrapper preserves behavior)
Unit test: Existing test_merge_slot_branches_recovers_from_partial_failure still passes

Known-bad: a resolver that simply trusts its return value (no MERGE_HEAD recheck) would let a silently-broken Claude resolution land on the main branch. The mock 'returns Resolved but did nothing' test catches this — a naive impl pushes to merged_slots and the test fails.
Failure mode: If `git rev-parse --verify MERGE_HEAD` itself fails to spawn (binary missing), treat as 'merge state unknown' → reset to pre_merge_head and push Failed with the spawn error
userStories.description:
Refactor merge_slot_branches in src/loop_engine/worktree.rs to take a resolver callback. The resolver is invoked when `git merge --no-edit <ephemeral>` exits non-zero; it returns an outcome enum that drives slot accounting. Production wires a real Claude-spawning resolver (FEAT-002); tests inject deterministic mocks.

DO:
- Add `pub(crate) enum MergeResolverOutcome { Resolved, Aborted, Failed(String) }` describing the three terminal states the resolver can produce.
- Add `pub(crate) struct ResolverContext<'a> { slot: usize, slot0_path: &'a Path, ephemeral_branch: &'a str, conflicted_files: &'a [String], pre_merge_head: &'a str }` carrying everything the resolver needs.
- Add `pub(crate) trait MergeResolver { fn resolve(&self, ctx: ResolverContext<'_>) -> MergeResolverOutcome; }` so tests can implement deterministic fakes.
- Add `pub(crate) fn merge_slot_branches_with_resolver(project_root, branch_name, num_slots, resolver: &dyn MergeResolver) -> MergeOutcomes` containing the body that today lives in merge_slot_branches.
- Make the existing `merge_slot_branches(project_root, branch_name, num_slots) -> MergeOutcomes` a thin wrapper that passes a no-op resolver returning `MergeResolverOutcome::Failed("no resolver".into())` for callers that don't yet wire one. Behavior is unchanged for those callers (today's reset path).
- Add helpers `fn list_conflicted_files(slot0_path: &Path) -> Result<Vec<String>, String>` (uses `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U`), `fn has_unresolved_merge(slot0_path: &Path) -> bool` (checks `git rev-parse --verify MERGE_HEAD`), and `fn rev_parse_head(slot0_path: &Path) -> Result<String, String>` (re-uses the existing `capture_head` if the signature matches; otherwise add).
- Inspection sequence on conflict: capture conflicted files → call resolver → re-inspect MERGE_HEAD/HEAD to validate the outcome the resolver claimed. If MERGE_HEAD still set → force `git reset --hard <pre_merge_head>` and treat as Failed regardless of resolver's claimed Resolved.

DO NOT:
- Change behavior for any existing call site in this task (they'll wire the real resolver in FEAT-003).
- Make the inspection helpers public outside the crate.
- Use stringly-typed outcomes (`String`/`bool` flags) instead of the enum.
- Skip the post-resolver MERGE_HEAD re-check — the resolver could lie or partially succeed.

Edge cases:
- Resolver returns Resolved but MERGE_HEAD is still set → coerce to Failed, reset, log discrepancy.
- Resolver returns Resolved and MERGE_HEAD cleared but HEAD didn't move → degenerate case, treat as Aborted.
- Resolver returns Aborted and HEAD == pre_merge_head → expected, push to failed_slots with 'Resolver declined' note.
- list_conflicted_files returns empty vec on a non-zero merge exit → defensive: still call resolver with empty list (it can `git merge --abort`).
- capture_head fails before merge → push current behavior (rev-parse failure entry in failed_slots), no resolver invocation.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A resolver-callback that bypasses MERGE_HEAD verification would silently accept incorrect resolutions. The contract is enforced by the OUTER inspection, not by the resolver's return value alone.
- Re-using a single `pre_merge_head` across slots is correct (the loop captures fresh per iteration), but resetting to it after a slot's PRIOR merge succeeded would undo that prior slot's work. Reset must use the head captured for THIS slot's attempt.
edgeCases: Resolver Resolved + MERGE_HEAD still set: coerce to Failed, reset, distinct diagnostic
Resolver Resolved + HEAD did not move: degenerate, treat as Aborted
Resolver Aborted + HEAD at pre_merge_head: expected; failed_slots with 'declined'
list_conflicted_files spawn failure: still attempt resolver with empty list (resolver may abort); never panic
capture_head fails: push existing rev-parse failure diagnostic, do NOT invoke resolver

failureModes.cause: Resolver claims Resolved but MERGE_HEAD remains
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Force git reset --hard pre_merge_head; push to failed_slots with explicit MERGE_HEAD diagnostic

failureModes.cause: git rev-parse --verify MERGE_HEAD fails to spawn
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Treat merge state as unknown; reset and push Failed with spawn error text

userStories.notes: Existing capture_head() at src/loop_engine/worktree.rs:502 may serve as rev_parse_head — re-use rather than add a parallel helper. The MergeOutcomes struct at line 496 is already pub(crate). Patterns for git Command invocation: src/loop_engine/worktree.rs:568 (the existing merge call). Test fixtures: src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs::setup_git_repo_with_file at line 245. Learning [1804]: ephemeral branch per slot — preserved as-is. Learning [1870]: deterministic naming via ephemeral_slot_branch. Test for the no-op wrapper: invoke merge_slot_branches (the back-compat name) on a conflicting fixture and assert the same failed_slots membership the original test produced.
qualityDimensions: Resolver contract enforced by external inspection (MERGE_HEAD + HEAD position), never by resolver's word alone
All git failures preserve stderr/stdout in the failed_slots diagnostic via format_git_failure
Reset uses each attempt's captured pre_merge_head, never a stale one
Existing `merge_slot_branches` callers see unchanged behavior — back-compat wrapper passes a deterministic 'no resolver' marker
Helpers return Result<_, String>, not panic; spawn errors flow into failed_slots as diagnostics
userStories.title: Add resolver-callback seam + post-merge inspection helpers

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `src/loop_engine/merge_resolver.rs` defines `ClaudeMergeResolver<'a>` and implements the `MergeResolver` trait from FEAT-001
Positive: `build_resolver_prompt(ephemeral_branch, conflicted_files)` returns a String containing the ephemeral branch name and every file from conflicted_files (literal substring match in tests)
Positive: `spawn_claude` is called with permission_mode == Auto, working_dir == slot0_path, model == self.model, effort == Some("medium")
Negative: `spawn_claude` is NEVER called with permission_mode == Dangerous (grep test or static check via clippy if feasible)
Negative: Empty conflicted_files short-circuits to Failed BEFORE spawn — no Claude invocation

Unit test: `build_resolver_prompt("feat-x-slot-1", &["src/foo.rs".into(), "src/bar.rs".into()])` contains 'feat-x-slot-1', 'src/foo.rs', 'src/bar.rs', and the strings 'git merge --abort', 'git commit', '<<<<<<<'
Unit test: prompt contains explicit prohibitions for 'git push', 'branch -d', 'reset --hard' outside the merge
Unit test: outcome inference (extracted as a pure function) maps (MERGE_HEAD=absent, HEAD!=pre) → Resolved
Unit test: outcome inference maps (MERGE_HEAD=absent, HEAD==pre) → Aborted
Unit test: outcome inference maps (MERGE_HEAD=present, *) → Failed
Unit test: empty conflicted_files returns Failed('no conflicts reported, refusing to spawn') without invoking spawn (use a builder fn that doesn't take a real Claude binary)
Known-bad: a resolver that calls spawn_claude unconditionally would burn API budget on degenerate inputs. The empty-list short-circuit test catches this.
Failure mode: spawn_claude returning Err propagates as MergeResolverOutcome::Failed with the IO error text
CONTRACT: SpawnOpts field names match EXACTLY src/loop_engine/claude.rs:90 (working_dir, model, effort, signal_flag, db_dir, timeout) — grep to verify
userStories.description:
Add a real resolver implementation that spawns Claude in slot 0's worktree to resolve merge conflicts. Lives in a new module so tests can exercise the prompt builder and outcome inference without spawning the binary.

DO:
- Create `src/loop_engine/merge_resolver.rs` (and add `pub(crate) mod merge_resolver;` to `src/loop_engine/mod.rs`).
- Define `pub(crate) struct ClaudeMergeResolver<'a>` carrying the loop's resolved model, db_dir, signal_flag, and a configurable `claude_timeout: Duration`.
- Implement `MergeResolver` (the trait from FEAT-001) for `ClaudeMergeResolver`.
- The `resolve` method:
  1. Build the prompt via `pub(crate) fn build_resolver_prompt(ephemeral_branch: &str, conflicted_files: &[String]) -> String`. Prompt content is documented inline (see below).
  2. Call `crate::loop_engine::claude::spawn_claude` with:
     - `permission_mode = PermissionMode::Auto { allowed_tools: None }`
     - `working_dir = Some(slot0_path)`
     - `model = Some(self.model.as_str())` (the loop's resolved default)
     - `effort = Some("medium")`
     - `timeout = Some(TimeoutConfig { base_timeout: self.claude_timeout, initial_extension: Duration::from_secs(0), last_activity_epoch: Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(0)) })`
     - `signal_flag = self.signal_flag`
     - `db_dir = self.db_dir`
     - everything else default
  3. After spawn returns, inspect HEAD and MERGE_HEAD via the helpers from FEAT-001 and translate to MergeResolverOutcome:
     - MERGE_HEAD absent + HEAD != pre_merge_head → Resolved
     - MERGE_HEAD absent + HEAD == pre_merge_head → Aborted (Claude ran `git merge --abort`)
     - MERGE_HEAD still set → Failed with reason from spawn (timed_out / non-zero exit / 'incomplete')
     - spawn_claude Err → Failed with spawn error
- The prompt MUST include: (a) the ephemeral branch name, (b) the bullet-list of conflicted files, (c) instruction to inspect via `<<<<<<<`/`=======`/`>>>>>>>` markers, (d) instruction to commit with `git commit --no-edit` on success or `git merge --abort` if unresolvable, (e) explicit DO NOT instructions for `git push`, branch deletion, or resetting outside the conflicted merge, (f) hard cap on response length to keep stream manageable.

DO NOT:
- Use `PermissionMode::Dangerous` — Auto is the ceiling.
- Allow the resolver to write outside slot0_path (set `working_dir` so Claude's sandbox enforces this).
- Add retry logic — one attempt per conflict; failure flows back to the loop.
- Hardcode the model — pull from the loop's resolved default (passed in via the struct).
- Spawn Claude on an empty conflicted_files list — short-circuit Failed with 'no conflicts reported' (defensive: shouldn't happen in practice, but the contract should be explicit).

Edge cases:
- Empty conflicted_files: short-circuit Failed('no conflicts reported, refusing to spawn'); no spawn.
- spawn_claude returns Ok with timed_out=true: Failed('Claude timed out after Xs') AND inspect MERGE_HEAD (Claude may have committed mid-timeout — though unlikely with SIGTERM, defensive).
- Claude exits 0 with MERGE_HEAD still set: Failed('non-zero merge state despite success exit') — coerce as the FEAT-001 contract demands.
- signal_flag tripped during resolve: spawn_claude already handles SIGTERM/SIGKILL; we surface as Failed('signal received during resolve').

Known-bad to guard against:
- A prompt that says 'fix all the issues you find' without scoping to MERGE_HEAD's conflicts would invite Claude to refactor unrelated code. Prompt MUST scope to the listed files only.
- Spawning without `working_dir` would let Claude write to the source repo's main worktree (or wherever the loop runs from). working_dir = slot0_path is mandatory.
edgeCases: Empty conflicted_files: short-circuit Failed without spawning
spawn_claude Err (binary missing): Failed with the IO error text
spawn_claude timed_out=true: Failed; still inspect MERGE_HEAD (defensive)
Claude exits 0 with MERGE_HEAD set: Failed (coerced) per FEAT-001 contract
Signal flag tripped mid-resolve: spawn_claude returns; outcome inferred from final state

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Return MergeResolverOutcome::Failed with the spawn error text

failureModes.cause: Claude introduces a regression in the resolution
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Out of scope for FEAT-002 — REVIEW-001 / next loop iteration handles. The resolution is committed and visible in `git log`.

userStories.notes: spawn_claude signature: src/loop_engine/claude.rs:253. SpawnOpts: src/loop_engine/claude.rs:90. PermissionMode enum: src/loop_engine/config.rs:206. TimeoutConfig: src/loop_engine/watchdog.rs (TimeoutConfig struct). Claude default model resolution: caller (FEAT-003) passes the resolved model in. For testing the prompt without spawning, isolate the prompt builder as a free function. For testing outcome inference, isolate the post-spawn inspection as a pure function `infer_outcome(merge_head_present: bool, head_changed: bool, spawn_result: SpawnResult) -> MergeResolverOutcome`. Learning [1828]: SpawnOpts is the clean abstraction — re-use it. Learning [1827]: TimeoutConfig pattern. Learning [1600]: cleanup_title_artifact is OPT-IN; do NOT enable it here (only curate uses it).
qualityDimensions: Prompt builder is a pure function returning String — testable without spawning anything
Outcome inference is a pure function on (merge_head_present, head_changed, spawn_result) — testable without git
Auto permission mode is enforced; Dangerous mode is statically unreachable from this module
Empty conflicted_files short-circuits before any I/O
Working directory is always the slot 0 path — Claude's sandbox enforces the boundary
userStories.title: Implement Claude-spawning merge resolver

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: engine.rs constructs `ClaudeMergeResolver` with params.model, params.signal_flag, params.db_dir, and a 600s timeout, then passes it to `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver`
Positive: When merge succeeds (no conflicts), resolver is never invoked (verified via existing tests; no Claude binary required)
Positive: Warning text reflects whether Claude was attempted vs pre-resolver failure
Negative: No hardcoded model string in engine.rs at the new call site (grep test: no `claude-sonnet-` / `claude-opus-` literal in the new lines)
Negative: No regression in `parallel_slots == 1` path (sequential mode still passes existing tests)
Unit test: build a ClaudeMergeResolver from a synthetic Params-like input; verify the model/timeout fields are propagated

Integration: in a multi-slot test fixture WITHOUT `claude` on PATH, force a conflict, run merge_slot_branches_with_resolver via engine, observe failed_slots with diagnostic mentioning 'spawn' or 'resolver' (binary missing path)

Known-bad: if engine still calls the back-compat `merge_slot_branches` (without _with_resolver), conflicts will silently fall through to the no-op resolver and lose work. Test by introducing a conflict and asserting the resolver was invoked (use a tracing flag or counter on a test-mode resolver wrapper).
Failure mode: if ClaudeMergeResolver construction itself fails (shouldn't, but defensive), engine logs and falls back to the no-op back-compat path so the wave still advances
userStories.description:
Replace the no-op resolver wrapper at the engine.rs call site (line ~1354) with a real `ClaudeMergeResolver` built from the loop's params. Update the warning text so users see whether Claude was attempted.

DO:
- In `src/loop_engine/engine.rs` around the existing call to `worktree::merge_slot_branches(params.source_root, params.branch, params.parallel_slots)`:
  - Build a `ClaudeMergeResolver` from `params` — model, db_dir, signal_flag, claude_timeout (use a 600s default — match the plan's '10 minutes').
  - Switch to calling `worktree::merge_slot_branches_with_resolver(...)` with that resolver.
- Update the failed_slots warning to indicate whether Claude was attempted: change the eprintln to read 'Warning: slot {} merge-back failed after Claude resolution attempt: {} (slot's commits remain on its ephemeral branch)' when the diagnostic indicates Claude was tried; keep the original wording when the diagnostic is a pre-resolver failure (e.g., spawn errors before the resolver was invoked). A simple discriminator: if the diagnostic contains 'resolver' or 'Claude', use the new wording.
- No change to `merge_slot_branches`'s back-compat signature; this task only changes the call site.

DO NOT:
- Re-add a `MergeResolverConfig` struct — the resolver itself carries its config (FEAT-002).
- Hardcode model strings — read from `params.model` (or the resolved default the engine already uses for waves).
- Skip the warning text update — the operator needs to know whether Claude was even tried, not just that the merge failed.
- Touch any code outside the call site at engine.rs:~1354.

Edge cases:
- params.model is None / empty: pass the resolved default (use the same lookup the wave uses for slot iterations); failing that, fall back to the SONNET_MODEL constant from src/loop_engine/model.rs.
- params.signal_flag already tripped before merge-back: the resolver propagates SIGTERM via spawn_claude; the outer post-spawn inspection still runs and produces a clean failed_slots entry.
- parallel_slots == 1: merge_slot_branches early-returns (existing behavior); resolver is never built/invoked. Verify no panic on this path.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Building the resolver and never using it (because parallel_slots == 1) is wasteful but harmless. The existing early-return in merge_slot_branches makes this benign — but verify by walking the code path.
edgeCases: params.model None/empty: fall back to resolved default → SONNET_MODEL last-resort
params.signal_flag tripped before merge-back: resolver still inspects post-spawn state
parallel_slots == 1: early return preserved; resolver unused

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Log error and call the back-compat merge_slot_branches; wave still advances

Default Claude model lookup pattern: see how select_next_task / spawn paths derive the model in engine.rs (likely via a helper). SONNET_MODEL constant: src/loop_engine/model.rs::SONNET_MODEL. Learning [1825]: slot-aware helpers exist; reuse them rather than re-deriving paths.
